name: Gigi Gryce
description: American recording artist; jazz saxophonist, flutist, clarinetist, composer, arranger, and educator (1925–1983)
abstract: Gigi Gryce (born George General Grice Jr.; November 28, 1925 – March 17, 1983), later in life changing his name to Basheer Qusim, was an American jazz saxophonist, flautist, clarinetist, composer, arranger, and educator. While his performing career was relatively short, much of his work as a player, composer, and arranger was quite influ…

concerts.label: Midnight Performance: Charlie Parker Memorial Concert

about_quotes.speaker: Mickey Roker
about_quotes.evidence: So then in 1959 I went to New York to play with Gigi Gryce. So I played with Gigi Gryce for a couple of years
about_quotes.doc_title: Mickey Roker interviewed by Michael Woods, Majesty of the Seas, May 31, 1995

about_quotes.speaker: Benny Golson
about_quotes.evidence: They heard Clifford. They heard me. They heard Gigi. ... Gigi Gryce, Art Farmer, Shahib Shihab. Yeah, that’s what it was. And Julius Watkins on trombone – uh, Jimmy Cleveland on trombone and Julius Watkins on french horn. That’s right. That was my first date as a leader.
about_quotes.doc_title: Benny Golson — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Art Farmer
about_quotes.evidence: I met Gigi in the band... That was a good time too. It was nice to meet these guys. We were like a family.
about_quotes.doc_title: Art Farmer — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Jon Hendricks
about_quotes.evidence: I did things for Gigi Gryce and Benny Golson too: Social Call and I Remember Clifford.
about_quotes.doc_title: Jon Hendricks — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Jacques Butler
about_quotes.evidence: INTERVIEWER: ...He and Gigi Gryce, I think they came over there maybe -- maybe, I'm not sure, they may have come through there with Lionel Hampton's band? [INTERVIEWEE] Well, probably, but see, I couldn't have heard them because I was working too much here.

about_quotes.speaker: JJ Johnson
about_quotes.evidence: The interviewer mentions Gigi Gryce in the context of other musicians (Benny Golson, Quincy Jones) who were influenced by the session, but JJ Johnson does not mention Gigi Gryce directly or describe a personal interaction.
about_quotes.doc_title: JJ Johnson — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
